assertions.title: Feature Queue mirrors `features.json` order, highlights current work, and marks validator features distinctly

steps.action: Open the existing Mission-GUI-TEST Mission session and switch to Mission Control.
steps.expected: Mission Control shows a feature queue for the active Mission session.
steps.observed: Mission Control rendered `mission-feature-queue` with the current validator-phase mission data.

steps.action: Compare the rendered queue order with `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/features.json`.
steps.expected: Rendered rows stay in the same order as `features.json`.
steps.observed: The live queue started with `user-testing-validator-mission-control-ui`, `mission-integration-tests-and-e2e-readiness`, then `mission-protocol-metadata-and-kill-worker-plumbing`, matching the live `features.json` ordering.

steps.action: Inspect the current row and validator rows in the live queue DOM.
steps.expected: Current work is highlighted and validator features are visually distinct from normal rows.
steps.observed: `mission-feature-user-testing-validator-mission-control-ui` rendered with the current highlighted styling, while validator rows such as `scrutiny-validator-mission-control-ui` remained visually distinct from standard completed/pending rows.

evidence.network: No relevant API/XHR requests were captured for the queue itself; reloads only fetched renderer assets from `http://localhost:5173/*`.

assertions.title: Validator injection does not prematurely mark the Mission complete

steps.action: Review the current Mission artifacts after implementation worker completion and validator injection.
steps.expected: The mission remains active while validator work is still pending.
steps.observed: Live artifacts showed `completedFeatures: 16`, `totalFeatures: 18`, `scrutiny-validator-mission-control-ui` completed, and `user-testing-validator-mission-control-ui` still in progress.

steps.action: Inspect the live Mission Control status and queue during the validator phase.
steps.expected: The UI does not show the Mission as finished after the first handoff or validator injection.
steps.observed: Mission Control still showed `Running` / `Mission is running`, with the user-testing validator highlighted as current and no completed-mission presentation.

steps.action: Cross-check the progress timeline against the mission progress log.
steps.expected: `worker_completed` and validation-trigger events are present without collapsing the Mission into a completed state.
steps.observed: The timeline and progress log both included `worker_completed` followed by validator-related events while the Mission surface stayed active.

evidence.network: No relevant API/XHR requests were captured; the validator-phase UI reflected renderer state restored from mission artifacts.

assertions.title: Mission status shows live Mission state and progress counts quickly

steps.action: Inspect the live Mission Control status card during the current validator phase.
steps.expected: Mission state and progress counts are visible in Mission Control.
steps.observed: The live view showed `Running`, `16/18 completed`, and the current feature as `user-testing-validator-mission-control-ui`.

steps.action: Point the isolated session to the copied mission snapshot and update `/tmp/droi-mission-control-sanity/state.json` with a new completed count/current feature while timing UI propagation.
steps.expected: Mission Control updates within 500ms when mission state/progress data changes.
steps.observed: The copied snapshot change from `10/13 completed` to `11/13 completed` appeared in the UI after 162ms.

steps.action: Cross-check helper coverage for Mission Control status derivation.
steps.expected: Focused automated tests cover live state/progress derivation for Mission Control.
steps.observed: `test/missionControl.test.ts` covers validator-aware status/progress summaries and completion gating logic.

evidence.network: No relevant API/XHR requests were captured; status/count updates were driven by Electron mission snapshot watchers.
evidence.timing: Copied snapshot progress-count update surfaced in 162ms.

assertions.title: Progress Timeline appends chronological Mission events in near real time

steps.action: Inspect the Mission Control timeline against the copied mission snapshot and progress log.
steps.expected: Timeline entries are timestamped and shown in chronological order.
steps.observed: Timeline rows rendered timestamp labels with ordered event labels matching the mission progress log sequence.

steps.action: Append a synthetic `worker_failed` entry to `/tmp/droi-mission-control-sanity/progress_log.jsonl` while timing the UI update.
steps.expected: A new timeline row appears within 500ms when the log grows.
steps.observed: The new `Synthetic append timing probe` event appeared in Mission Control after 200ms.

steps.action: Reload the copied snapshot and verify timeline persistence and dedupe support.
steps.expected: Appended entries persist across reload, and duplicate-entry dedupe is covered.
steps.observed: The synthetic timeline event remained visible after reload, and `test/missionControl.test.ts` explicitly verifies duplicate progress entries collapse to a single rendered item.

evidence.network: No relevant API/XHR requests were captured; timeline updates were triggered by mission snapshot file changes in the isolated copy.
evidence.timing: Copied snapshot progress-log append surfaced in 200ms.

assertions.title: Worker handoff cards appear, survive validator work, and recover after restart

steps.action: Inspect the live validator-phase Mission Control handoff area.
steps.expected: Worker handoff cards are visible while validator work continues.
steps.observed: The live validator-phase session still showed `Worker handoffs` while `user-testing-validator-mission-control-ui` was in progress.

steps.action: Open recovered handoff cards and verify required fields.
steps.expected: Each handoff card includes summary, implementation details, and verification results.
steps.observed: Recovered handoff cards exposed `mission-handoff-*` test ids plus Summary, What was implemented, Verification commands, and Interactive checks content.

steps.action: Reload the copied mission snapshot after appending timeline data and verify handoff recovery.
steps.expected: Existing handoffs reappear after reload/restart recovery from disk.
steps.observed: After reload, the copied snapshot still showed handoff cards such as `mission-protocol-metadata-and-kill-worker-plumbing` and `mission-page-chat-shell-and-view-toggle`, confirming persistence/recovery from disk.

evidence.network: No relevant API/XHR requests were captured; handoff recovery was driven by mission artifact reload/reconciliation.

frictions.description: After full-session reloads, the Electron UI briefly showed an empty/no-project state before the Mission session rehydrated from disk; this required waiting about 10 seconds before capture-ready Mission Control evidence returned.
frictions.resolution: Waited for restore to settle, then re-snapshotted the same assigned session before collecting persistence evidence.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Live Mission Control matched validator-phase queue/status behavior, copied-snapshot timing probes updated within 162ms/200ms, and handoff/timeline recovery persisted across reload.
